title: LedgerProof Corporate Operations State
doctrine: Operational and execution state for the company. Distinct from lpr-status.json (protocol state) and win-conditions.json (quarterly boolean win conditions). This file captures phase, load-bearing corrections, active workstreams, kill switches, and the current operating reference document.

foundation_institutional_engagement.eu_ai_office_consultation_filed: 2026-06-02 (Article 50 Guidelines draft consultation; sections II/III/IV/VI/VII; anchored Foundation receipt at 17-futurium/anchored-receipts/2026-06-02-article-50-consultation/)
foundation_institutional_engagement.foundation_interim_key_disclosure: Interim Ed25519 key created 2026-06-02 for governance event anchoring prior to Foundation root-key ceremony Aug 15, 2026. Will be rotated to 2-of-3 multisig via key-transition receipt signed by interim key, establishing unbroken authority chain.
foundation_institutional_engagement.ietf_scitt_draft_status: draft-dawkins-scitt-ai-article50-00 published on Datatracker (WG adoption pending — NOT yet WG-adopted, keep distinction crisp)
foundation_institutional_engagement.regulator_briefings_held: 0 — Hallensleben + Toffaletti + Birkholz cold outreach pending warming via consultation submission credential

load_bearing_corrections.statement: Article 95 confers reputational signal only — NOT presumption of conformity. Only Article 40 harmonized standards published in the Official Journal under M/593 confer presumption of conformity.

Kong / Envoy sit on outbound enterprise API publication. Snowflake UDFs / Databricks notebooks / Airflow operators / Prefect tasks are the inbound third-party-data verification surface. Two distinct architectures, two distinct plugin paths.

LangChain Inc. and LlamaIndex Inc. are commercial entities. Engagement model is integration partnership (with their core teams), not fractional-equity committer recruitment. Off-market equity + employer conflict-of-interest both apply.
Receipt verification must be LOCAL (Merkle proof + Ed25519 signature + Bitcoin anchor — all offline-verifiable). LedgerProof verification nodes are an OPTIONAL CONVENIENCE, not a requirement.

Enterprise compliance dashboard = customer's SIEM (Splunk / Datadog / ServiceNow / Vanta / Drata / custom SIEM). /v/{slug} is the LedgerProof PUBLIC verifier portal. Two distinct surfaces; two distinct integration paths.

Kong body_filter kills streaming AI latency (50-200ms p99). Stream-aware signing required: commit on stream-start, sign on stream-end. HSM per-request signing is prohibitive at AI inference volumes; Merkle-batch architecture (N requests → 1 HSM signature) required.

LangChain has migrated to LangGraph for production agents. Integration targets BOTH callback handlers (legacy inference loops) AND LangGraph node middleware (current agentic workflows). Receipts emit to a side channel (log/queue/webhook) — callback handlers cannot modify response payloads.
Tencent parent ownership of Riot Games creates EU-press vulnerability. A US or EU counterweight anchor customer must confirm in the same window as Riot before Riot becomes the public launch story.

active_workstreams.name: Regulatory / Standards
active_workstreams.lead: Veronica + fractional EU regulatory affairs strategist

active_workstreams.name: Security / Audit
active_workstreams.lead: Veronica + NCC + ToB + Cure53 + Senior Security Advisor

KS1_AUDIT_ENGAGEMENT.trigger: No NCC Group + Trail of Bits engagement letters returned
KS1_AUDIT_ENGAGEMENT.branch: Switch to Cure53-only + Latacora contingency; extend audit-memo publication +30 days; tranche-trigger Sep 30 becomes conditional on Cure53 memo only.

KS2_SEED_CAPITAL_FLOOR.trigger: Less than $8M raised at seed close
KS2_SEED_CAPITAL_FLOOR.branch: Drop one of: senior protocol engineer hire, Brussels counsel retainer, or full audit. Default drop: Brussels counsel — keep audit + senior engineer.

KS3_TENCENT_LEAK_PREVENTION.trigger: Riot/Tencent press leak before US/EU counterweight anchor confirms
KS3_TENCENT_LEAK_PREVENTION.branch: Brunswick crisis response within 4 hours; Foundation independence FAQ deployed; founder media silence until counterweight confirms.

KS4_DIN_APPLICATION_DEADLINE.trigger: DIN SME membership application not filed
KS4_DIN_APPLICATION_DEADLINE.branch: Switch primary standards path to AFNOR (France) or NEN (Netherlands); both have member-org NWIP authority. Cost: ~4 weeks of standards-timeline delay.

KS5_KONG_PLUGIN_SHIP.trigger: Kong plugin v0.1 not open-sourced by Aug 2 (Article 50 enforcement)
KS5_KONG_PLUGIN_SHIP.branch: Ship Snowflake UDF + LangChain integration first; defer Kong to Q4 2026; reframe enterprise narrative around data-plane (not API-plane) per C2.

KS6_ENFORCEMENT_SOFT.trigger: Zero visible Article 50 enforcement action by Aug 16 (T+14 days post-enforcement)
KS6_ENFORCEMENT_SOFT.branch: Activate second-narrative track: pre-drafted op-ed published; commercial narrative shifts to 'voluntary adoption now reduces future enforcement exposure'.

KS7_LANGCHAIN_PARTNERSHIP_REFUSED.trigger: LangChain Inc. partnership conversation declines integration
KS7_LANGCHAIN_PARTNERSHIP_REFUSED.branch: Ship as community integration outside main; pursue LlamaIndex Inc. partnership as primary; reset adoption-curve assumption -6 months.

KS8_FORM_1023_DEFICIENCY.trigger: IRS Form 1023 returned with substantive deficiency
KS8_FORM_1023_DEFICIENCY.branch: Adler & Colvin response within 14 days; fiscal sponsorship via New Venture Fund continues as bridge; charitable contributions flow uninterrupted.

review_protocol: Friday 17:00 PT weekly review of phase status, kill-switch status, and workstream momentum. Updates committed Sunday evening for the upcoming week. Quarterly anchor as a Foundation receipt at the same cadence as win-conditions.json (first Tuesday after Sep 1, Dec 1, Mar 1, Jun 1).
